title: senecio pygmophyllus
intro: Senecio pygmophyllus, also known as Pygmy Groundsel, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Senecio pygmophyllus include its potential to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs, and soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Senecio pygmophyllus can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for oral consumption, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using the plant for medicinal purposes.
